purpose: Canonical product, feature, route-group, competitor, upgrade, and certification registry. Every shipped route group must be owned by exactly one product surface.
category_definition: Kolm is the verifiable AI compiler and artifact contract layer: traces and specs become portable signed artifacts with eval gates, receipts, runtime targets, and governance.
state_of_art_principles: Every product promise must have a route or artifact surface, owner, source docs, competitor context, and certification gate.
Every route group must be mapped exactly once to a product surface.
Every authenticated production surface must have an explicit prod smoke command before it can be called final.
Every competitor claim must resolve to primary docs or a refreshed source register entry.
Every launch blocker must be represented as code, config, or a named non-code credential dependency.

research_references.title: OpenAI supervised fine-tuning
research_references.state_of_art_signal: Provider-hosted fine-tuning can create adapted models but remains provider-bound; Kolm must win on portability, receipts, local execution, and cross-runtime packaging.

research_references.title: OpenAI prompt caching
research_references.state_of_art_signal: Provider caching reduces repeated prompt cost; Kolm must prove avoided calls and deterministic local hot paths, not just cache discounts.

research_references.title: Anthropic prompt caching
research_references.state_of_art_signal: Prompt-cache block control is now standard for long-context apps; Kolm routing should preserve provider cache fallbacks while compiling stable repeated tasks.

research_references.title: LangSmith evaluation concepts
research_references.state_of_art_signal: Datasets, experiments, traces, and online evaluators are table stakes; Kolm should import these signals and bind them into K-score evidence.

research_references.title: LangSmith observability
research_references.state_of_art_signal: Trace every call, step, and decision; Kolm receipts and trace export must interoperate with this workflow.

research_references.title: Arize Phoenix
research_references.state_of_art_signal: OpenTelemetry-based traces and eval integrations are standard; Kolm should emit and ingest OpenTelemetry/OpenInference-compatible traces.

research_references.title: W&B Weave
research_references.state_of_art_signal: Tracing, evaluation, and improvement loops are bundled in modern LLM platforms; Kolm must bind improvements to portable artifacts.

research_references.title: Predibase fine-tuning overview
research_references.state_of_art_signal: LoRA fine-tuning and multi-adapter serving are mature; Kolm should treat training backends as pluggable while owning artifact proof and runtime packaging.

research_references.title: Together AI fine-tuning
research_references.state_of_art_signal: Managed LoRA and full fine-tuning are widely available; Kolm should not compete on generic training UX alone.

research_references.title: OpenPipe fine-tuning quick start
research_references.state_of_art_signal: Trace-to-fine-tune workflows are an adjacent wedge; Kolm must make trace imports and eval gates first-class.

research_references.title: Amazon Bedrock model customization
research_references.state_of_art_signal: Managed distillation and fine-tuning are cloud-native enterprise expectations; Kolm should support BYOC/backend adapters and stronger artifact governance.

research_references.title: WorkOS docs
research_references.state_of_art_signal: SSO, SCIM, admin portal, and audit logs are expected for enterprise identity; Kolm account/team surfaces must converge there.

research_references.title: Auth0 Organizations
research_references.state_of_art_signal: B2B org modeling is a product primitive; Kolm must separate org, workspace, user, and tenant semantics.

research_references.title: Stripe Billing
research_references.state_of_art_signal: Self-serve subscription, invoice, and customer portal flows are table stakes; Kolm must move beyond payment-link-only certification.

research_references.title: Stripe customer portal
research_references.state_of_art_signal: Customer-managed billing is expected; Kolm account billing should expose portal and webhook-backed entitlement state.

research_references.title: Apple Core ML
research_references.state_of_art_signal: On-device runtime targets are platform-native; Kolm should emit/verifiably wrap runtime-specific assets instead of pretending to replace them.

research_references.title: Apple Foundation Models
research_references.state_of_art_signal: On-device foundation model APIs raise the offline bar; Kolm needs proof, evals, receipts, and governance above native model access.

research_references.title: Google LiteRT
research_references.state_of_art_signal: Android and edge deployment should target established runtimes; Kolm should be compiler/governance layer.

research_references.title: ONNX Runtime Mobile
research_references.state_of_art_signal: Cross-platform mobile inference requires model/runtime optimization; Kolm artifact manifests must name target runtime and proof.

research_references.title: PyTorch ExecuTorch
research_references.state_of_art_signal: Edge PyTorch deployment is a real substrate; Kolm should target it as an export backend.

surfaces.name: Identity, Access, Teams, And Billing
surfaces.promise: A buyer can sign up, authenticate, manage organizations and keys, change plans, and prove entitlement state without support intervention.
optimal_spec: Separate org, workspace, user, tenant, service account, and API-key identities.
Require server-validated credentials for all authenticated certification.
Expose billing portal, invoice state, entitlement, usage, quota, and webhook audit state.
Support SSO, SCIM, RBAC, key scopes, key rotation, and admin audit logs before enterprise-final claims.

local_gates: local anon bootstrap plus account smoke
blockers: payment-link billing must be backed by portal and webhook entitlement checks

surfaces.name: Public Site, Docs, API Reference, And SDK Distribution
surfaces.promise: Every public page, API reference, SDK asset, and route contract is current, crawlable, coherent, and hash-verifiable.
optimal_spec: Docs are generated from source route inventory and OpenAPI has zero undocumented route flags.
SDK assets are content-addressed and SRI-checked.
Public comparison pages use source-backed claims and never overstate live functionality.
Every public claim links to either a product route, a verifier, or an explicit roadmap status.

certification.slo: 99.9% public docs and SDK asset availability
blockers: direct production docs asset smoke must be added to product-surface verifier

surfaces.name: Compile, Artifact, Registry, Receipts, And Verification
surfaces.promise: A task can become a signed, portable, content-addressed artifact with K-score, receipt chain, registry metadata, and offline verification.
optimal_spec: Artifact manifests bind source, evals, K-score, provenance, runtime target, signature, CID, and optional transparency-log state.
Every publish path enforces productionReady unless explicitly forced with an auditable reason.
Registry and marketplace rows distinguish verified, yanked, deprecated, and manual-review states.
Cloud compile must be durable across restarts before enterprise-final claims.

prod_gates: marketplace publish dry-run or sandbox smoke
certification.slo: 99% compile control-plane availability; artifact verification must be deterministic
blockers: durable cloud compile and object storage are not proven from this audit

body.task: surface smoke: classify invoice approval emails

surfaces.name: Runtime, Inference, Connectors, And Multimodal APIs
surfaces.promise: Kolm can sit in the hot path as an OpenAI/Anthropic-compatible runtime, local artifact runner, and connector gateway with receipts and fallback behavior.
optimal_spec: Hosted runtime requires tenant auth; local daemon mode remains explicit and isolated.
Every connector records provider, model, latency, cost, prompt hash, privacy scan, and receipt metadata.
Prompt caching and provider fallback are preserved as fallbacks, while stable tasks can route to local artifacts.
Sandboxed recipe execution must be resource-limited and backed by a documented threat model.

certification.slo: p99 runtime control-plane latency under 500 ms for metadata routes
blockers: production readiness reports optional model_provider false

surfaces.name: Capture, Datasets, Evals, Labels, Training, And Improvement Loop
surfaces.promise: Production traces and user data can become governed datasets, labels, evals, simulations, distill runs, and safer follow-up artifacts.
optimal_spec: Every imported or captured row carries tenant, namespace, privacy mode, source system, checksum, retention, and purge metadata.
Eval cases are versioned, split, and linked to trace provenance before they affect K-score.
Distill, bakeoff, simulation, and training plans can be run in dry-run and production modes with cost bounds.
Improvement loops never overwrite a production artifact without K-score, drift, and rollback evidence.

certification.slo: 99% capture ingest availability; zero cross-tenant dataset leakage
blockers: external trace importer coverage remains roadmap unless fixtures are wired

surfaces.name: Governance, Compliance, Admin, Audit, Privacy, Trace, And Notifications
surfaces.promise: Operators can prove who did what, what data moved, what artifact ran, what privacy controls applied, and what compliance package can be exported.
optimal_spec: Audit logs are append-only, signed, exportable, and scoped by org/workspace/tenant.
Privacy scans can block, redact, hash, or allow by policy with explainable class-level evidence.
Compliance packages bind receipts, manifests, audit rows, DSR status, subprocessors, and retention policy.
Notifications and admin actions are rate-limited, tenant-fenced, and auditable.

certification.slo: audit export and privacy policy routes must never expose cross-tenant data
blockers: SOC2/HIPAA claims require external control evidence beyond code

surfaces.name: Deployment, Edge Devices, BYOC, Storage, Sync, Tunnel, And Federated Learning
surfaces.promise: Artifacts can move across cloud, BYOC, device, tunnel, sync, confidential-compute, and federated workflows without losing tenant boundaries or evidence.
optimal_spec: Device detection returns partial structured results instead of 500 when local probes fail.
Runtime targets name exact backend, quantization, hardware, expected latency, and verification status.
BYOC and tunnel deployments separate control plane from customer data plane and preserve receipt evidence.
Federated flows exchange hashes, approvals, and aggregates without leaking raw tenant data.

certification.slo: device and deployment control-plane routes must degrade gracefully and never leak cross-tenant state
blockers: BYOC/sync/tunnel/federated lifecycle smoke still requires live production credentials
